---
# Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation.
# Licensed under the MIT License.

name: Update CI Dependencies
description: >-
  Refreshes pinned CI/CD dependencies – SHA-pinned GitHub Actions in
  .github/workflows/, task versions and 1ES template refs in
  .github/azure-devops/ – and audits Node.js runtime consistency across
  workflows, pipelines, package.json, and .nvmrc. Triggered when
  Release – Initiate completes so updates land on the release pull request
  it opens; workflow_dispatch is available as a manual retrigger while a
  release pull request is open.

on:
  workflow_run:
    workflows:
      - Release – Initiate
    types:
      - completed
    branches:
      - "**"
  workflow_dispatch: null

permissions:
  contents: read
  pull-requests: read
  actions: read

engine: copilot

tools:
  edit:
  bash:
    - git *
    - gh api *
    - gh pr list *
    - gh pr view *
    - jq *

checkout:
  fetch:
    - *
  fetch-depth: 0

safe-outputs:
  push-to-pull-request-branch:
    target: *
    labels:
      - release
    max: 1
    if-no-changes: ignore
    protected-files:
      policy: fallback-to-issue
      exclude:
        - package.json
        - .nvmrc
    github-token-for-extra-empty-commit: ${{ secrets.PR_METRICS_TOKEN }}
---

# Update CI Dependencies

Refresh pinned versions in GitHub Actions workflows and Azure DevOps pipelines,
and enforce Node.js runtime consistency across workflows, pipelines,
`package.json`, and `.nvmrc`.

## Invocation Context

- Triggered by `Release – Initiate` completing or by a manual
  `workflow_dispatch`.
- Target: the open pull request that carries the `release` label (created by
  `Release – Initiate`, with title `[Autogenerated] Release v<version>`).
- If no pull request with the `release` label is currently open, stop and
  produce no output. Do not open a new pull request.

## Inventory

Catalog every pinned version before editing. Use `grep` to locate each pattern.

### GitHub Workflows

Files under `.github/workflows/*.yml`:

- **SHA-Pinned Actions**: `uses: owner/repo@<40-char SHA> # vX.Y.Z`. The SHA and
  the trailing version comment must stay in sync.
- **Other Pinned Tools**: Literal `version:` or `ref:` values in step inputs.

### Azure DevOps Pipelines

Files under `.github/azure-devops/*.yml`:

- **Task Versions**: Entries of the form `task: TaskName@N` (for example,
  `Npm@1`, `UseNode@1`, `EsrpCodeSigning@6`). Only the major version is
  declared; the latest minor or patch resolves at runtime.
- **Template Refs**: `ref: refs/tags/<tag>` inside `resources.repositories`. 1ES
  templates use the moving `release` tag; pin a specific tag only when
  compliance requires it.

## Process

1. Confirm a single open pull request labelled `release` with the
   `[Autogenerated] Release v` title prefix exists. If none matches, stop and
   produce no output.
1. For each SHA-pinned GitHub Action, resolve the latest release and tag SHA:

   ```bash
   gh api repos/<owner>/<repo>/releases/latest --jq '.tag_name'
   gh api repos/<owner>/<repo>/git/refs/tags/<tag> --jq '.object.sha'
   ```

   Update the SHA and the trailing `# vX.Y.Z` comment in one edit – never one
   without the other. If the tag points to an annotated tag object, dereference
   it with a second `gh api` call against the tag object.

1. For each Azure DevOps task, check the task's latest major version in the
   [Azure Pipelines task reference][ado-tasks]. Bump the major version only
   after reviewing its release notes and adjusting inputs accordingly. This
   includes `UseNode@1` itself (the task version), but never the `version:`
   input it receives.
1. For template refs, list available tags and compare with the current pin:

   ```bash
   git ls-remote --tags <repo-url>
   ```

   The 1ES templates are hosted in Azure DevOps; authenticate via `az` if the
   listing is restricted.

1. Save changes while preserving formatting: trailing newlines, comment spacing,
   and two-space indentation.

## Node.js Version Consistency

The pinned Node.js runtime must match everywhere it appears. This workflow does
not bump Node.js to a newer version – that is a deliberate, standalone change –
but it flags and resolves inconsistencies.

Locate every occurrence with `grep`:

- `.github/workflows/*.yml` – `node-version: X.Y.Z` under `actions/setup-node`.
- `.github/azure-devops/*.yml` – `UseNode@1` with `version: X.Y.Z`.
- `package.json` – `engines.node`.
- `.nvmrc` if present.

If values diverge, align them on the single intended pin. Prefer the value
already set by the most recent deliberate bump (check `git log` on whichever
file changed most recently). Do not change the value itself.

## Invocation-Specific Overrides

- **Commit Message**: Use `chore: refresh CI pins`, not a
  dependency-class-specific message, because a single invocation may span
  multiple classes.
- **Output Target**: Push onto the existing release pull request's branch. The
  `push-to-pull-request-branch` safe output in the frontmatter handles this – do
  not call `gh pr create` yourself.

## Constraints

- **Never Desynchronize SHA and Version Comment**: Always update both in the
  same edit.
- **Never Blindly Bump an Azure DevOps Task Major Version**: Review the release
  notes and adjust inputs first.
- **Never Bump the Node.js Runtime Value**: Audit only. A runtime bump is a
  separate, deliberate change made elsewhere.
- **Never Duplicate a Dependabot Pull Request**: If Dependabot already has an
  open pull request for an action, rebase or merge it rather than producing a
  competing change.
- **Never Hard-Pin a 1ES Template Ref Without Justification**: The `release` tag
  is intentionally moving.
- **Never Modify Files Outside the Allowed Set**: Only
  `.github/workflows/*.yml`, `.github/azure-devops/*.yml`, `package.json`, and
  `.nvmrc` may be edited.

## Quick Reference

| Dependency Type   | Files                        | Update Source          |
| ----------------- | ---------------------------- | ---------------------- |
| GitHub Action     | `.github/workflows/*.yml`    | `gh api` latest tag    |
| Azure DevOps Task | `.github/azure-devops/*.yml` | ADO task reference     |
| 1ES Template Ref  | `.github/azure-devops/*.yml` | `git ls-remote --tags` |

## Verification

No manual verification is required. After the push, the `Build` workflow reruns
automatically (via the empty commit made with `PR_METRICS_TOKEN`) and
re-executes Super-Linter against the modified files. Any lint or typing failure
surfaces there.

[ado-tasks]: https://learn.microsoft.com/azure/devops/pipelines/tasks/reference/
